title: MotifGate: a PWM-aware residual learning model for TFBS prediction
description: MotifGate is an interpretable, lightweight model for transcription-factor binding-site (TFBS) prediction in sequence windows of K = 7-14 bp. It combines an exact, differentiable position-weight-matrix (PWM) prior with a learned residual branch modulated by a single trainable scalar gate. This formulation renders the magnitude of the learned correction relative to the interpretable prior directly measurable. The MotifGate architecture is compact (approximately 37,000 trainable parameters) and was trained and evaluated on CPU. This record archives the reproducible software package, comprising the model implementation, the training and evaluation pipeline, leakage-controlled split construction, sampling of negatives from real binding sites without synthetic sequences, interpretability and calibration analyses, the UniBind external-validation pipeline, and the statistical-significance, gate-extraction, and ablation analyses. All data derive from publicly available resources (JASPAR and UniBind); no new experimental data were generated.
